name: Beef and Barley Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut meat into 3-inch cubes. Trim and discard any excess fat.
2. In a large saucepan or soup kettle, combine the meat, water, bay leaf, and peppercorns. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 1 hour, skimming off foam occasionally.
4. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, cover barley with water. Bring to a boil, then drain and rinse under cold running water. Discard the hot water.
5. Add the rinsed barley along with celery, carrots, leeks, onion, and rutabaga to the soup pot. Return to a simmer and cook for about 30 minutes or until barley is tender.
6. Ladle into bowls. Garnish with parsley.
8 servings/serving size: 1/8 recipe
NOTE: Some cookbooks may refer to this as barley broth. It is a full-bodied, economical medley of vegetables, bits of meat, and barley that makes a great, satisfying meal on its own.
NOTE: Beef and Barley Soup (Scotch Broth -- British)
